# Swarm Stall: Mutual Idle State without Heartbeat Mitigation

## Context
During swarm operations, if both agents (e.g., Claude Code and Antigravity) complete their respective tasks and conclude their turns by yielding to the human commander (without dispatching an A2A message), the swarm enters a mutual idle state.

## The Problem
Because the current wake substrate (Phase 3) is driven primarily by inbound message events (`SENT_TO_ME`, `TASK_STATE_CHANGED`), an absence of messages results in a "Swarm Stall." Neither agent receives a wakeup event to autonomously re-evaluate the queue or pick up the next task. The entire swarm halts until a human commander manually intervenes with a prompt.

## Proposed Solution
We need a fallback mechanism for mutual idle states:
1. **Idle-Timeout Watchdog:** The Memory Core could implement a watchdog that monitors the last activity timestamp. If X seconds pass without agent activity, it dispatches a "Heartbeat" or "Queue Re-eval" wake event to a designated orchestrator agent.
2. **Daemon Heartbeat:** A lightweight script (e.g., `swarm-heartbeat.sh`) that periodically polls the Memory Core and forces a wakeup if the swarm is stalled and tickets remain in the queue.

*This ticket tracks the design and implementation of the Swarm Stall mitigation (Phase 4 / Heartbeat).*

> ◆ ## Substantively addressed by #10671 substrate; integration outstanding
> 
> Investigated 2026-05-05. Same finding as my comment on sibling #10396 (filed within minutes of this one):
> 
> This ticket's "Daemon Heartbeat" proposed solution (option 2 in body: *"a lightweight script (e.g., `swarm-heartbeat.sh`) that periodically polls the Memory Core and forces a wakeup if the swarm is stalled and tickets remain in the queue"*) is **already implemented** as `ai/scripts/swarm-heartbeat.sh` under [Epic #10671](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/10671)'s 9 closed sub-issues.
> 
> The script EXISTS as a continuous-loop daemon. What's missing is **persistent-process management** (launchd plist / systemd unit / equivalent) to actually keep it running. Plus the `wakeSafetyGate` is currently `tripped` (operator-set 2026-05-03 22:53Z), which would no-op high-authority recovery actions even if the daemon were running.
> 
> The mutual-idle-stall failure mode this ticket names was **empirically demonstrated this very session** — multiple times, all 3 agents idled out simultaneously while the gate-tripped state prevented heartbeat-driven recovery from firing. @tobiu caught and named it on each occurrence.
> 
> **Recommend close as `RESOLVED_BY_RELATED` (#10671)** once #10671 epic-finish lands (plist + gate-untrip + end-to-end validation). Detailed integration close-out matrix in [my comment on #10671](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/10671#issuecomment-...).
